data.title: Texas Instruments shares fall for seven consecutive sessions
data.description:
Texas Instruments (TXN) shares clocked seven straight sessions of losses on Friday, as the stock was 2.3% lower at $193.34.

The analog semiconductor company lost nearly 7% in the preceding six sessions.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, TXN has a Hold rating with a score of 3.3 out of 5. The company received A in the prospect of profitability, while it received C- in the growth factor.

Seeking Alpha analysts are cautious and see the stock as a Hold.

Turning to the Wall Street, 14 analysts have given the stock a Buy or above rating. 18 gave the stock hold recommendation, while five gave Sell or lower rating.

In February, Texas Instruments (TXN) signed a definitive agreement to acquire Silicon Labs (SLAB) for around $7.5B.

 
 

“Despite strategic logic, TXN shares remain fairly valued post-deal, with limited near-term upside and better risk/reward opportunities elsewhere,” pointed out a Seeking Alpha analysis.

data.title: NI Holdings reports Q4 results
data.description:
• NI Holdings press release (NODK):  Q4 Net investment income increased 6.9% to $11.7 million, driven by the favorable interest rate environment and increased average fixed income securities balance, offset by lower interest rates earned on cash and cash equivalents.
  • Loss per share of $(0.15) for the current year quarter compared to earnings per share of $0.47 for the prior year quarter, and loss per share of $(0.50) for the current year compared to earnings per share of $0.31 for the prior year.
  • Q4 net earned premiums of $58.2 million, down 18.9% compared to prior year quarter, and full year net earned premiums of $270.7 million, down 12.7% compared to prior year.

data.title: Offerpad receives NYSE notice for sub-$1 share price, plans steps to regain compliance
data.description:
• Offerpad Solutions (OPAD) on Friday said it received a notice from the New York Stock Exchange stating the company is not in compliance with listing rules after its Class A common stock averaged below $1.00 over 30 consecutive trading days.
  • The company said the notice does not result in immediate delisting, and its shares will continue to trade on the NYSE during the compliance period.

  • Offerpad said it notified the exchange on March 5 that it intends to cure the deficiency and can regain compliance within six months if its closing share price and 30-day average closing price both reach at least $1.00 by the last trading day of any month during that period.

  • The company said it will consider available alternatives, including a potential reverse stock split subject to shareholder approval at its next annual meeting, if necessary to regain compliance.
  • OPAD +0.73% after hours to $0.7509.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Wearable Devices announces 1-for-3 reverse stock split
data.description:
• Wearable Devices (WLDS) announced on Friday that it intends to effect a one-for-three reverse split of the company’s ordinary shares and its tradable warrants (the “Warrants”).
  • The ordinary shares and warrants will continue to trade on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the existing symbols “WLDS” and “WLDSW,” respectively, and will begin trading on a split-adjusted basis when the market opens on March 11, 2026. 
  • The primary purpose of the reverse share split is to increase the per-share trading price of the company's ordinary shares to regain compliance with the $1.00 minimum bid price requirement for continued listing on the Nasdaq. 
  • The reverse share split will adjust the number of issued and outstanding ordinary shares of the company from 10,593,227 ordinary shares to approximately 3,531,076 ordinary shares and the number of publicly held warrants from 98,589 warrants to approximately 32,863 warrants.
  • WLDS -5.92% after hours to $0.7452.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Harvard Bioscience announces 1-for-10 reverse stock split
data.description:
• Harvard Bioscience (HBIO) said on Friday it has approved a 1-for-10 reverse stock split of the company’s common shares, following authorization from shareholders at a special meeting held on March 6, 2026.
  • The reverse stock split will take effect on March 13, 2026, intended at increasing the company’s share price to regain compliance with the Nasdaq Capital Market’s minimum bid requirement.
  • No fractional shares will be issued in connection with the reverse split.
  • The transaction will reduce the company’s outstanding common shares to about 4.47 million from roughly 44.72 million, while the total authorized share count will remain unchanged.
  • Shares -4.19%.

data.title: U.S. drilling count rises for first time in four weeks, Baker Hughes says
data.description:
The number of oil and gas rigs actively drilling in the U.S. added 1 to 551, rising for the first time in four weeks, Baker Hughes said Friday in its latest weekly survey.

During the week ended March 6, the number of active drilling rigs targeting crude oil in the U.S. rose by 4 to 411, the highest level since early February, gas rigs dropped 2 to 132, and 8 rigs were classified as miscellaneous.

The total rig count was down by 41, or 6.9%, from the same time last year, with oil rigs down 75, or 15.4%, and gas rigs up 31, or 30.7%, compared to a year ago.

The number of rigs targeting oil in the Permian Basin gained 2 to 241, the oil rig count in the Eagle Ford jumped by 4 to 32, and the Williston Basin lost 1 rig to 27.

ETFs: (USO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

data.title: Powell Industries announces three-for-one stock split
data.description:
• Powell Industries (POWL) has approved a three-for-one forward stock split of the company’s common stock. The company also approved a proportionate increase in the number of authorized shares of common stock to accommodate the stock split. 
  • The company expects that the stock split will increase the number of shares of the company’s outstanding common stock from ~12.1 million shares to ~36.4 million shares. 
  • POWL +0.15% after hours to $483.3.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Methode targets $950M–$1B sales with 50% data center run rate growth while advancing transformation
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Methode Electronics (MEI) Q3 2026

Management View

  • Jonathan DeGaynor, President and CEO, opened by highlighting the resilience of Methode's global team in a "challenging and rapidly evolving environment," noting the company's ongoing multiyear transformation journey. He emphasized, "We generated $234 million in sales and $7.3 million in adjusted EBITDA. While profitability was pressured year-over-year, we delivered positive free cash flow of $10 million in the quarter and approximately $17 million in year-to-date cash flow as we remain on track to achieve our fiscal '26 free cash flow targets."
  • DeGaynor reported a 9.5% year-over-year increase in Industrial segment sales, primarily from off-road lighting and power distribution supporting data centers. He attributed this to "growing exposure to higher-growth industrial power markets and helps offset some of the headwinds we are seeing in North American automotive and in commercial vehicle lighting."
  • The CEO described ongoing transformation efforts, including stabilizing operations, portfolio refinement, and cost structure alignment, specifically citing a "completed sale of the dataMate business," headquarters relocation, and a purchase agreement on a former facility. He stressed, "These are deliberate, measurable actions, and we are doing what we said we would do."
  • DeGaynor pointed to Power Solutions as a core focus, revealing a "line of sight toward $120 million annualized run rate" for data center revenue in Q4, representing a "significant increase in run rate year-over-year" and a projected "50% increase in run rate year-over-year in the near term."
  • Laura Kowalchik, CFO, stated, "Third quarter net sales were $233.7 million compared to $239.9 million in fiscal 2025, a decrease of 3%." She added, "Gross profit was $38.8 million, down from $41.3 million in the prior fiscal year quarter." She highlighted positive free cash flow and a year-to-date figure of $16.5 million. "Net debt was down $16.9 million compared to the same period last year."

Outlook

  • Kowalchik announced narrowing of fiscal 2026 net sales guidance to $950 million to $1 billion, raising the low end of the range, "primarily reflect[ing] the benefit of foreign currency translation, which totaled approximately $25 million through the first 9 months."
  • Adjusted EBITDA outlook was lowered to $58 million to $62 million, citing "multiple customer program delays and higher expenses associated with the transformation of our Mexico facility, including wages and professional fees."
  • The company "continue[s] to expect positive free cash flow in the fourth quarter and for the full year compared to an outflow of $15 million in the previous fiscal year."

Financial Results

  • Net sales for the quarter were $233.7 million, compared to $239.9 million in the prior year quarter.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $7.3 million, down $5 million year-over-year.
  • Adjusted net loss was $13.1 million, a $5.9 million change from the prior year.
  • Adjusted loss per diluted share was $0.37 compared to $0.21 in the prior year.
  • Free cash flow for the quarter was $10.1 million, and $16.5 million year-to-date.

Q&A

  • John Franzreb, Sidoti: Asked about the Mexico transformation timeline. DeGaynor responded, "the transformation in Mexico is probably about 6 months behind where we are with Egypt...we have not seen the productivity improvements as quickly as we initially expected."
  • Franzreb, Sidoti: Queried on commercial truck order impact. DeGaynor replied, "Both what we've seen from DTNA and Paccar is more of second half of calendar '26 as to where the volumes start to come back...we aren't yet seeing it."
  • Franzreb, Sidoti: Inquired about dataMate revenue and profitability. DeGaynor confirmed, "it's roughly $18 million worth of revenue. It was profitable. But what I can say is roughly $3 million worth of profitability."
  • Luke Junk, Baird: Sought clarification on data center run rate and CapEx. DeGaynor explained, "This run rate that we're talking about is that transparency. This is backed with EDI...a Q4 run rate of $120 million with EDI that gives us great confidence."
  • Junk, Baird: Asked for base number for 50% growth. DeGaynor replied, "$80 million to $85 million as a basis in our guidance."
  • Junk, Baird: Queried about Mexico launches and delays. DeGaynor noted, "we've spent the money...to prepare for the launches, and we've had further delays even from what we said in the last quarter."
  • Gary Prestopino, Barrington: Asked about EV program cancellations. DeGaynor stated, "we have talked about some Stellantis program cancellations as well as other programs that are delayed."
  • Prestopino, Barrington: Questioned EV take rates. DeGaynor said, "the answer is yes. And it's primarily in North America."
  • Franzreb, Sidoti: Asked about program launches. DeGaynor said, "Our number was 29 programs in this fiscal year...the number is still the same. It's just a question of whether we got the revenue from it."
  • Franzreb, Sidoti: Asked about portfolio review. DeGaynor answered, "dataMate was an important first step...The portfolio review was ongoing, and you can expect more to come."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed concern about Mexico’s lagging transformation, program delays, and the impact of EV headwinds in North America, with repeated questions about timing, profitability, and growth drivers.
  • Management maintained a determined and disciplined tone in prepared remarks, but acknowledged challenges and delays, frequently using phrases such as "we have not seen the productivity improvements as quickly as we initially expected" and "there are factors outside of our near-term control."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst tone was more pressing and focused on underlying operational issues and growth confidence, while management was slightly more defensive, particularly around Mexico and program delays.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Sales guidance was narrowed upward, but adjusted EBITDA guidance was lowered from $70-80 million previously to $58-62 million, driven by ongoing North American auto headwinds and Mexico transformation costs.
  • Strategic focus remains on transformation, cost control, and capitalizing on data center and industrial power growth.
  • Analysts shifted questions from stabilization progress to specifics on delayed launches, program cancellations, and data center growth certainty.
  • Management's confidence in industrial and data center growth was more heavily emphasized, while defensiveness around North American auto and Mexico challenges increased.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited "commercial vehicle market softness, EV program delays and macro volatility, particularly in North American automotive" as key risks.
  • Ongoing transformation of the Mexico facility and associated costs are impacting near-term profitability.
  • Program delays and cancellations, especially in North American EVs, are weighing on expected revenue.
  • Portfolio review is ongoing, with further divestitures possible.
  • Management is prioritizing operational execution, cost control, and capital allocation to mitigate volatility.

Final Takeaway

Methode Electronics emphasized ongoing transformation, including cost structure alignment, portfolio refinement, and operational improvements, while facing headwinds in North American automotive and delays in Mexico facility productivity. The company raised the low end of its full-year sales guidance to $950 million, highlighted strong momentum in its data center business with a $120 million annualized run rate and a projected 50% near-term growth, and signaled further portfolio actions ahead. Management remains focused on strengthening the foundation and generating consistent free cash flow, aiming for improved long-term performance despite current volatility in key end markets.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: OpenAI and Oracle decide not to expand Texas data center site: report
data.description:
OpenAI (OPENAI) and Oracle (ORCL) have decided not to expand their data center site in Abilene, Texas, from 1.2 GW to 2 GW, according to Bloomberg.

Instead, Meta Platforms (META) is now considering stepping in to lease the planned expansion, which is owned and being developed by Crusoe, the report said, citing people familiar with the matter. Nvidia (NVDA) also played a role in steering Meta to take advantage of the expansion.

Apparently, Oracle and OpenAI's expansion plans were hampered by financing issues and changes to forecasted demand, the report said.

The Abilene data center is part of the Stargate Project, and construction continues on the 1,000-acre development. Both Oracle and OpenAI are continuing to utilize the Stargate site.

"We are very proud of our relationship and our progress in bringing capacity online," Oracle said in a statement, according to the report.

"Together, Crusoe and Oracle are operating in lockstep to deliver one of the world's largest AI factories in Abilene," Crusoe added. "Our collaboration can deliver massive-scale infrastructure faster than anyone else in the industry."

Meanwhile, Meta continues to push full-steam ahead with its lofty data center goals. Last month, the social media giant entered into a multi-year, multi-generation agreement with AMD (AMD) to deploy up to 6 GW of AMD Instinct chips to power Meta's next generation of AI infrastructure.

data.title: Just how high will gas prices climb with oil now above $90/bbl
data.description:
Escalating tensions in the Middle East are beginning to ripple through U.S. energy markets, with consumers likely to feel the impact at the gas pump in the weeks ahead.

Crude oil (CL1:COM) prices surged above $90 per barrel on Friday as the conflict between the United States and Iran intensified, pushing energy markets to their highest levels in more than two years. Rising crude prices typically feed directly into gasoline costs, raising concerns about higher fuel bills for households and businesses. 

Prediction markets are already signaling a sharp move higher in gasoline (XB1:COM) prices. Current forecasts suggest there is a near-certain probability that the national average price will push above $3.00 per gallon in March. Some projections indicate prices could climb significantly higher, with scenarios placing gasoline above $4.20 per gallon if oil continues to rally. 

See below what Kalshi traders are predicting when asked: How high will gas prices go in the U.S. this month?

  • Above $3.00 — 99%
  • Above $3.10 — 97% 
  • Above $3.20 — 95%
